prompt: Collections.sort throws ClassCastException. What is the bug?
choices: Collections.sort requires an explicit Comparator for any List
Widget does not implement Comparable<Widget>; sort attempts to cast elements to Comparable and fails with ClassCastException
Collections.sort cannot be called on custom objects
Widget must override equals() and hashCode() to be sortable
explanation: Collections.sort(list) requires elements to be Comparable. Widget doesn't implement it, so at runtime the cast to Comparable throws ClassCastException. Fix: either implement Comparable<Widget> in Widget, or pass a Comparator: Collections.sort(widgets, Comparator.comparingInt(w -> w.id)).
